tasks.title: Project scaffolding & Colab-ready environment setup
acceptance_criteria: Repository folders created: data/, notebooks/, src/, models/, reports/ (data/ and models/ gitignored)
requirements.txt (or environment.yml) with pinned versions for transformers, datasets, torchaudio or librosa, evaluate, jiwer, accelerate
A Colab-ready notebook skeleton (notebooks/00_setup.ipynb) that: mounts Google Drive, verifies GPU/T4 availability, installs pinned dependencies
.gitignore covering data/, models/, checkpoints, .ipynb_checkpoints, __pycache__
README.md documenting how to open and run the setup notebook in Google Colab
tasks.progress: Scaffold complete: folder layout, .gitignore, pinned requirements.txt, Colab setup notebook (Drive mount, GPU check, pip install, import smoke test), README with Colab open/run instructions. Local pip+import validation passed on Python 3.12; Colab T4 end-to-end run pending human verification.

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All acceptance criteria met. Colab E2E not run by Cursor — recommend human runs 00_setup.ipynb with GPU before T-003.

test_results.colab_e2e_gpu: not_run — requires fresh Colab session with GPU runtime; human should Run all on 00_setup.ipynb

tasks.title: Data acquisition & preprocessing pipeline for OpenSLR SLR54
acceptance_criteria: Script/notebook downloads or ingests OpenSLR SLR54
Audio resampled to 16kHz mono; transcripts NFC-normalized
Utterances filtered to 0.5s < duration < 30s
Speaker-disjoint 80/10/10 train/val/test split produced and saved
Split manifest files (csv/jsonl) committed under data/ (metadata only, not raw audio)
tasks.progress: SLR54 pipeline implemented in src/ (download, preprocess, speaker-disjoint split, manifests) + notebooks/01_data_prep.ipynb. TSV ingest validated (157,905 utts / 527 speakers). Synthetic local run produced committed manifests under data/manifests/. Full 16-zip preprocess requires Colab run (~8 GB).

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All 5 acceptance criteria met; splits.py verified correct (speaker-level partitioning, disjointness asserted, seeded); pytest 7/7 independently re-run and passed. Synthetic-data validation approach disclosed transparently, not hidden. gitignore nested-exception fix from T-001 review confirmed correct. Full SLR54 zip run deferred to Colab per T-001 constraints — acceptable.

test_results.spot_check_audio: passed — 3 files @ 16kHz mono, duration in bounds, NFC normalized
test_results.full_slr54_zip_pipeline: not_run — ~8 GB download; run notebooks/01_data_prep.ipynb in Colab

tasks.title: Whisper-Small fine-tuning script/notebook on Colab T4
acceptance_criteria: Notebook loads openai/whisper-small and WhisperProcessor
Seq2SeqTrainingArguments configured for T4 (batch size 2, grad_accum 4, lr 1e-4, warmup 500, max_steps 2000, eval_steps 200)
Checkpoints saved to Google Drive at save_steps intervals and resumable after disconnect
Training run completes at least one full evaluation cycle with logged loss/eval curves
tasks.progress: Revision v8: consolidated training notebook to notebooks/02_finetune_whisper.ipynb (inference demo + pip install folded in); deleted root whisper_small.ipynb. Core: src/training.py, smoke eval at step 3, checkpoint-3 saved.

notes: 2026-07-03: Core implementation verified correct — eval_loss=0.806/eval_wer=0.667 in checkpoint-3/trainer_state.json matches reported values exactly, 6/6 tests pass independently, checkpoint reloads successfully. First submission requested changes for a duplicate root-level notebook.

2026-07-03 (revision): Approved. Confirmed root whisper_small.ipynb deleted, single notebooks/02_finetune_whisper.ipynb retains the inference-demo cell (folded in, not dropped) plus pip-install cell. Re-ran pytest tests/test_training.py independently — 6/6 passed. All 4 acceptance criteria met.

test_results.checkpoint_reload: passed — model.safetensors present at checkpoint-3 (967 MB)
test_results.colab_full_training: not_run — run notebooks/02_finetune_whisper.ipynb on Colab T4 with SMOKE_TEST=False
test_results.revision_notebook_consolidation: passed — root whisper_small.ipynb deleted; single notebook under notebooks/

tasks.title: Evaluation pipeline: WER/CER on in-domain and out-of-domain sets
acceptance_criteria: Evaluation script computes WER and CER using jiwer/evaluate on the SLR54 held-out test set
Same script evaluates on at least one out-of-domain set (Common Voice Nepali or FLEURS ne_np)
NFC normalization applied identically to reference and hypothesis before scoring
Results exported as a table (csv/markdown) under reports/
tasks.progress: src/evaluation.py + notebooks/03_evaluate.ipynb. WER/CER via jiwer with shared NFC normalization. Out-of-domain: FLEURS ne_np. Smoke results on 3-step checkpoint exported to reports/.

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All 4 acceptance criteria met. Verified src/evaluation.py reuses src/preprocessing.py::normalize_transcript_nfc (not reimplemented) and applies it symmetrically to both reference and hypothesis in compute_wer_cer — the exact pitfall start.md §5.3 warns about. Independently re-ran pytest tests/test_evaluation.py — 5/5 passed. reports/wer_cer_results.{csv,md} content matches reported test_results exactly, and the markdown report correctly flags the numbers as smoke-checkpoint sanity checks, not real results — good judgment given the underlying model is only 3 training steps in.

test_results.sanity_not_trivial: passed — metrics neither 0%/0% nor broken pipeline
test_results.note: Smoke checkpoint (3 training steps); numbers are pipeline sanity checks only

tasks.title: Error analysis and report-artifact generation
acceptance_criteria: Qualitative sample set of reference vs hypothesis transcripts exported under reports/
Error categorization covering phonetic confusion, OOV/rare vocabulary, noise degradation, code-switching per start.md §3.3 checklist
Plots/tables suitable for direct inclusion in the Results & Discussion report section
tasks.progress: src/error_analysis.py + notebooks/04_error_analysis.ipynb. Per-utterance ref/hyp samples and category heuristics (start.md §3.3). Exported error_samples, error_categories md/csv/png under reports/.

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All 3 acceptance criteria met. src/evaluation.py was refactored to expose UtterancePrediction/collect_manifest_predictions for reuse by error_analysis.py rather than re-transcribing from scratch — verified this refactor didn't break anything by re-running the full suite (test_data_prep + test_training + test_evaluation + test_error_analysis = 18/18 passed). reports/error_samples.{csv,md} and error_categories.{csv,md,png} content matches reported test_results exactly (4 utterances, oov_rare_vocabulary=4, dialect_accent=4), correctly caveated as smoke-checkpoint-only. Minor non-blocking nit: categorize_errors()'s final if/else both append 'other' (redundant, harmless). This completes the T-001..T-005 backlog; a full Colab GPU training run remains a human-executed next step.

test_results.note: Smoke checkpoint; categories degenerate until full Colab training

tasks.title: Full-pipeline QA pass (T-001..T-005)
acceptance_criteria: Full test suite (test_data_prep.py, test_training.py, test_evaluation.py, test_error_analysis.py) re-run from a clean state and all pass, with the pass count reported

Each notebook (00_setup.ipynb, 01_data_prep.ipynb, 02_finetune_whisper.ipynb, 03_evaluate.ipynb, 04_error_analysis.ipynb) opens as valid notebook JSON and its Python imports/paths are checked for correctness (static check acceptable where a live Colab GPU run isn't available)
requirements.txt installs cleanly in a fresh environment and every pinned package importable

README.md, project-context.md folder-structure section, and any in-repo docs are cross-checked against the actual repo layout for stale references (e.g. no leftover mentions of the deleted root whisper_small.ipynb, correct notebook filenames/order)
Repo hygiene check: git-tracked files reviewed for anything that shouldn't be committed (raw audio, __pycache__, .DS_Store, large binaries) and .gitignore coverage confirmed sufficient

A QA summary report is produced (e.g. reports/qa_summary.md) listing what was checked, pass/fail per item, and any issues found — issues found should be filed as blockers, not silently fixed outside this task's scope unless trivial and clearly in-scope (e.g. fixing a stale doc reference)
tasks.progress: Full QA pass complete: 23/23 tests, 5 notebooks validated, fresh venv install OK, docs cross-checked, trivial fixes applied (README matplotlib, .pytest_cache gitignore, Agg backend, stale log ref). reports/qa_summary.md produced.

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All 6 acceptance criteria met. Independently re-ran full suite (MPLBACKEND=Agg pytest tests/) — 23/23 passed, matching reported count. Verified all three trivial fixes are genuinely present: matplotlib added to README deps list, .pytest_cache/ added to .gitignore, Agg backend set in error_analysis.py. Confirmed .gitignore correctly covers __pycache__/.DS_Store/data/*/models/* (on-disk cruft from local dev, not git-tracked). implementation-log.md history was appended to, not rewritten. reports/qa_summary.md is an honest, non-fabricated checklist. No blockers filed, none needed.

tasks.title: Streamlit visualization dashboard for evaluation/error-analysis reports
acceptance_criteria: A Streamlit app (dashboard/app.py) launches locally via `streamlit run dashboard/app.py` without error
Displays the WER/CER results table from reports/wer_cer_results.csv (in-domain and out-of-domain rows)
Displays qualitative error samples from reports/error_samples.csv with a filter/selector by error category
Displays the error category breakdown (reuse reports/error_categories.csv, either embed reports/error_categories.png or regenerate an equivalent chart inline)
Displays training loss/eval curves parsed from a checkpoint's trainer_state.json log_history (e.g. models/whisper-small-ne-smoke/checkpoint-3/trainer_state.json)
Dashboard clearly labels current data as smoke-checkpoint sanity-check results, not final report numbers, consistent with T-004/T-005's reporting convention
README.md updated with a section on how to install (streamlit added to requirements.txt) and run the dashboard locally

A basic smoke test confirms the dashboard module imports cleanly and its data-loading functions (reading the reports/ CSVs and trainer_state.json) work without error — full Streamlit UI interaction testing is out of scope
tasks.progress: dashboard/app.py + dashboard/data.py: 4-tab Streamlit viewer (metrics, samples, categories, curves). Reads existing reports/ CSVs and trainer_state.json. Smoke-checkpoint warning banner. README + requirements updated.

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All 8 acceptance criteria met. Independently re-ran pytest tests/test_dashboard_data.py — 7/7 passed. Read dashboard/data.py and dashboard/app.py in full: clean separation of pure data-loading (data.py, no Streamlit imports) from rendering (app.py), matching the requested architecture. Went further than the packet's own claim ('subprocess headless start attempted') by actually launching `streamlit run dashboard/app.py --server.headless true` and confirming HTTP 200 from a live server — genuinely works end-to-end, not just statically parsed. Smoke-checkpoint warning banner present and prominent. Sidebar checkpoint-path override is a nice touch beyond the minimum ask. Process cleaned up after verification.

test_results.streamlit_startup: AST parse + import path verified; subprocess headless start attempted
test_results.data_loaders: wer_cer, error_samples, error_categories, trainer_state.json all parse OK

tasks.title: Live microphone voice-to-text demo tab in Streamlit dashboard
acceptance_criteria: A new 'Live Demo' tab is added to dashboard/app.py using Streamlit's native st.audio_input widget (no new heavy dependency like streamlit-webrtc) to record microphone audio in-browser

Recorded audio is converted/resampled to 16kHz mono as needed before inference, reusing existing conversion logic (e.g. src/preprocessing.py or src/evaluation.py transcribe_array) rather than duplicating it
Inference runs via the currently-loaded checkpoint (reuse src/evaluation.py::load_checkpoint / transcribe_array) and displays the transcribed Nepali text in the UI

The tab clearly labels output as coming from the current checkpoint (default: the T-003 smoke checkpoint) and sets expectations that transcription quality will be poor/garbage until a full Colab training run replaces it — same caveat convention as the other tabs
No-audio or recording-error states (e.g. user hasn't recorded anything yet, or transcription raises an exception) are handled gracefully without crashing the app

A smoke test covers the underlying transcription call path (not st.audio_input itself, which can't be automated in a unit test) using a sample synthetic audio file consistent with existing test patterns in tests/test_dashboard_data.py or tests/test_evaluation.py
README.md's dashboard section is updated to mention the Live Demo tab and that it requires browser microphone permission
tasks.progress: Live Demo tab in dashboard/app.py using st.audio_input; dashboard/inference.py for bytes→array + transcribe helpers; reuses load_checkpoint/transcribe_array; cached model via st.cache_resource; graceful None/error states.

notes: Approved 2026-07-03. All 7 acceptance criteria met. Independently re-ran pytest tests/test_dashboard_inference.py + test_dashboard_data.py — 11/11 passed, matching packet. dashboard/inference.py correctly delegates resampling to src/evaluation.py::transcribe_array rather than reimplementing it. Live Demo tab correctly uses the shared sidebar checkpoint_dir (not hardcoded), st.cache_resource kept in app.py (not leaked into the Streamlit-free inference.py), broad try/except around transcription showing st.error instead of crashing, plain prompt for the audio_input-is-None state. Live-verified: launched `streamlit run dashboard/app.py --server.headless true` on port 8503 with the new tab present, curl returned HTTP 200, process cleaned up after. README updated with mic-permission note. Uses st.audio_input per ADR-003 — no new heavy dependency added.

test_results.no_audio_state: verified via None branch — prompt shown, no transcription attempted
test_results.transcription_smoke: transcribe_wav_bytes + transcribe_audio_file return str against smoke checkpoint

tasks.title: Ingest full Colab training results & finalize report artifacts
tasks.human_prerequisite: Complete Colab runbook steps 0–6 (full SLR54 data prep, 2000-step training, eval, error analysis, dashboard checkpoint pointed at real model). Human returns with 'check' and reported WER/CER.
acceptance_criteria: Real training checkpoint path documented (default: models/whisper-small-ne/ or best eval checkpoint subdirectory) and synced from Drive to local models/ if not already present

reports/wer_cer_results.{csv,md} regenerated from notebooks/03_evaluate.ipynb against the full checkpoint — in-domain SLR54 test WER/CER and out-of-domain FLEURS ne_np WER/CER present; smoke-checkpoint caveat removed or replaced with final-results label
reports/error_samples.{csv,md} and error_categories.{csv,md,png} regenerated from notebooks/04_error_analysis.ipynb using the full checkpoint — non-degenerate category distribution on test set

Dashboard default checkpoint path in dashboard/app.py or README updated to the full checkpoint (or clearly documented one-line override); smoke-checkpoint warning banner updated to reflect final vs interim results
Full pytest suite still passes (MPLBACKEND=Agg pytest tests/) after any code/doc changes

A brief results summary added to reports/ (e.g. reports/training_results_summary.md) with: checkpoint used, train/val/test manifest sizes, final in-domain WER/CER, out-of-domain WER/CER, training steps completed, and whether results meet the ~15–30% in-domain WER target from project-context.md
